capabilities: Implement a systematic approach to detect performance regressions by benchmarking current software against a baseline, leveraging task decomposition for detailed analysis.

procedure.action: Capture baseline performance metrics using Apache JMeter and store results in a time-series database like InfluxDB.

Decompose tasks using agent.planning.task_decomposition to identify critical performance paths using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Run performance tests on new software versions using the same JMeter scripts and compare results against the baseline using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Set up Grafana dashboards to visualize performance differences and alert when deviations exceed 5% of baseline metrics.

Assert procedure output by cross-referencing with historical performance data and adjust thresholds as necessary by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

summary: Establish a robust framework for detecting performance regressions by comparing current software performance against a defined baseline using task decomposition strategies.
title: Baseline Comparison Benchmarking for Performance Regression Detection

failureConditions: Performance regressions go undetected due to inadequate baseline metrics.
False positives occur due to overly sensitive thresholds.

Task decomposition fails to identify critical performance paths; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: procedure output by cross-referencing with historical performance data and adjust thresholds as necessary by asserting a
successConditions: Performance tests consistently detect regressions exceeding 5% deviation from baseline; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Grafana alerts trigger correctly when performance deviates from baseline; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Task decomposition accurately identifies and logs critical performance paths; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
